programme.desc: Debbie has a close encounter when she works late at the bookies. Phil is suspicious about his brother, but Grant has Sharon on his mind. Ricky makes Bianca an offer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Honey is horrified to learn Matt has taken over all the wedding arrangements and warns Emma not to rush blindly into marriage. Sam angles for a date with Phil and Stuart is told to mind his own business

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: An offer of promotion for Ric comes at the wrong time. However, his emotional state does help him find a solution to Jayne's problem with accepting the money from Randall for the Tans' treatment. Maria is unsure how to react to a strange e-mail, Joseph confronts Jac over her meddling in his wedding plans, and Donna develops career ambitions

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Colette tells Simon he is responsible for their child's genetic abnormalities and decides to keep the baby after Comfort offers her support, but a nasty jolt in the back of the ambulance prompts her to go for an ultrasound scan. Nikki finds a compromise between work and play and Vinnay harasses Jack for the £300,000 Eddie owed him

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates the disappearance of a prostitute whose evidence helped put away rapist Martin Delaney. Zain finally persuades Kristen Shaw to name her supplier and puts himself in great danger to arrange a meeting with the mysterious Jose Alvarez. Phil apologises to Sam for missing their date and agrees to take things slowly, but Stuart makes it clear he's not going to be pushed out without a fight

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the day after Grant and Sharon's date. Carol and Alan are still having problems with Bianca and Peggy accepts Sharon's offer for the Vic

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Debbie has a close encounter when she works late at the bookies. Phil is suspicious about his brother, but Grant has Sharon on his mind. Ricky makes Bianca an offer

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Most TV characters are incapable of straying very far from their native haunts without either finding romance or stumbling over a murder and Dr Finlay is no exception. Here, his much-needed Highland walking holiday ends in the former, but it still turns out to be pretty hectic. David Rintoul stars

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: The sickly folk of Cardale are hit by yet another mystery illness, which sweeps through the village spreading terror in its wake _ and poor old Andrew, whose only desire is to get away from it all, finds himself drawn into deadly danger. Gary Mavers stars in the medical drama

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James performs an unfamiliar operation on a pedigree bull and helps his partners treat Mrs Bond's cats. Yorkshire veterinary drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester joins a sponsored cycle race to Brighton in aid of the RSPCA, but risks finishing up in worse shape than most of the animals she is trying to help. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: After moving into a double room, Tom and Diana bicker about whose furniture they should use. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: It's Rene's birthday and surprises include a car from Edith, a wink from Hitler and a bomb from the Resistance. The celebration concludes with a bang

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry's promotion at the building society gives him a headache when he has to visit a particularly violent family - and he naively asks Clegg and Truly for advice. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Mike Grady

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Customs seize a massive heroin shipment and Kavanagh agrees to prosecute the importer, a self-made man with all the trappings of success, including a yacht and a villa in Portugal. Unfortunately, he also has a staff of hired heavies who make the mistake of threatening Lizzie in a bid to get him off the hook - thus setting the scene for a bitterly personal courtroom wrangle. John Thaw and Lisa Harrow star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 16-year-old case of an infamous getaway driver convicted of murdering his wife when new evidence suggests it could have been a miscarriage of justice. But Gerry, who was on the original investigation, refuses to believe the prisoner is anything but guilty. Meanwhile, Brian is in a reflective mood, convinced his days at Ucos are numbered after his actions against former police commander Embleton. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star as the veteran coppers, with Amanda Redman as their boss

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zafar and Adam race against the clock to uncover the latest bomb, while the power struggle between Juliet and Harry intensifies. As a new ultimatum is issued and Tash mysteriously disappears, the team is forced to deal with the possibility of a mole within the organisation. Guest starring Martine McCutcheon and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates the disappearance of a prostitute whose evidence helped put away rapist Martin Delaney. Zain finally persuades Kristen Shaw to name her supplier and puts himself in great danger to arrange a meeting with the mysterious Jose Alvarez. Phil apologises to Sam for missing their date and agrees to take things slowly, but Stuart makes it clear he's not going to be pushed out without a fight

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: During a Union ball, the Green girls and Frank initiate a plan to help Tom escape. Dr Foster guides Samuel through an operation

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star

programme.desc: Kerri Davidson starts work at the Beeches, and Joanna is reunited with an old friend, while Andrew intervenes to solve Sam's accommodation dilemma

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets face the consequences of attacks by a stray dog on a flock of expectant sheep. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Carol Drinkwater

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: The couple return from a second honeymoon in Italy, but there is trouble ahead when lingerie is found in William's cas. Meanwhile, Guy and Nancy make a big announcment. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Jane discovers Diana using a slingshot armed with acorns to feed squirrels. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The British airmen are captured while paddling down the estuary, so Rene and the Resistance launch a cunning plan to rescue them - using a couple of coffins

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly scares Billy Ingleton out of his wits by mischievously starting a rumour there is a tiger on the loose. The story spreads like wildfire, leading to unexpected consequences for the practical-joking pensioner. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Frank Thornton and guest Norman Wisdom

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective investigates the meticulously planned murder of an elderly birdwatcher, and begins to suspect that he is on the trail of a vengeful serial killer. However, the case is jeopardised when he becomes too close to one of the key suspects. Drama, starring Kenneth Branagh, Sarah Smart, David Warner, Tom Hiddleston, Saskia Reeves and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Brian spends time at home making peace with himself over his dismissal, he agrees to help Esther's friend Margaret find her missing brother Peter, in the interest of keeping his mind active. Peter was the accountant of a man who has gone to prison for the murder of his wife, so Brian visits the convict hoping he will be able to assist. However, his investigation begins to encroach on the Ucos team's efforts to track down a conman who may be connected to several outstanding cases. With Rosalind Ayres and Art Malik

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: After the defection of a well-known MP, Adam and Fiona go undercover to infiltrate his new party - a right-wing extremist group known as The British Way - whose members plan to wreck the nation's democracy. Drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and David Threlfall, with Rupert Penry-Jones and Olga Sosnovska

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A young tearaway is brought in for questioning about a break-in at a doctor's surgery, but it soon becomes clear a staff member has something to hide. Yvonne loses confidence about her chances of landing the sergeant's job at Sun Hill and decides to look elsewhere for promotion. Emma chooses the wrong moment to tell Matt about her night of passion with Will

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: The unexpected arrival of a hospital inspector throws the staff into disarray, and while Mary empathises with a deserter, Silas makes Samuel pay for his arrogance

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric

Ric has to make some tough decisions about redundancies - with Lola top of the list

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Simon is called to the scene of an emergency and soon finds his future in jeopardy when his personal feud with Josh spills over into his professional life. Lara and Luke become the latest topic of staff gossip, Bex seizes the opportunity to flirt with Dillon, and Jack has another confrontation with Vinnay as the pressure mounts for him to come up with the money

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zain is troubled on the anniversary of his sister's drug-related death but decides to continue with his undercover operation, even though it means lying to Kristen. Will assumes Matt was behind his beating and throws a punch at him - only to end up being arrested by Tony for his actions. Yvonne impresses Heaton by getting a result in the break-in case involving Billy Aldridge

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca confides in David and is surprised by his attitude. Sharon is taken aback by Grant's plans. Ian decides it's time for a change

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark confesses his worries to Michelle over Ruth. Bianca thinks hard about the meaning of fatherhood, and Debbie finally snaps at Dougan

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: A patient's deathbed murder confession presents Dr Finlay with a crisis of conscience, over whether to keep a secret entrusted to him in strictest confidence or see to it that justice is done

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew encounters a patient whose sister is unwittingly killing him with kindness, while Sam encourages a grieving husband to pick up the pieces of his shattered life and accept responsibility for his baby daughter _ in spite of her heartbreaking legacy. On the personal front, Joanna confides in Carol about her one-night stand at the college reunion. Rural drama, starring Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan has a mishap while treating a cow, leaving the Darrowby practice facing a possible lawsuit. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William is headhunted by a French accountancy firm, and he and Hester decide to move to France. However, the pair soon discover that what divides the British and French is more than just the English Channel. Sequel to Fresh Fields, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom grows tired of watching Geoff fill every available space with shelves, and Diana feels restricted in her new role as nanny and decides to return to Bayview. Meanwhile, a postcard from Marion makes Geoff realise how much he misses her. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen prepare to meet their maker as they face a firing squad, while Madame Fanny discovers Edith in bed with the Fuehrer

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley finally finishes his off-the-road motorised scooter, while bored son-in-law Barry longs for a walk on the wild side. Rural comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Thora Hird

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth uncovers a dark secret when a contestant is murdered at a ballroom dancing competition. Drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of pornographer Jim Hockney is reopened when his daughter Sara provides DNA proof he wasn't her father. The team tries to track down jazz singer Angela Gold, who was the victim's girlfriend at the time and disappeared from the music scene after being acquitted of the crime, and it's Brian's replacement Danny Griffin (Nicholas Lyndhurst) who manages to find her. However, with Angela professing her innocence and Sara's mother Jane also arousing suspicion, it becomes a struggle to determine who is telling the truth. With a guest appearance by former Corrie killer Brian Capron

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: MI5 discovers a notorious Islamic terrorist is planning to attack the UK, so Adam and Zafar travel to Turkey in a bid to persuade him to join them instead. Despite the apparent success of the mission, the team is uncertain whether the new recruit means exactly what he says. Drama, starring Rupert Penry-Jones and Raza Jaffrey

programme.title: Gregory's Girl (1981)
programme.desc: A gawky teenager is dismayed to be relegated to goalkeeper in the school football team - but his thoughts turn to romance once he meets the female striker who takes his place. However, his awkward nature proves an obstacle to winning her over, despite the sage advice of his worldly-wise little sister. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: People who have ended up out of pocket after being caught out by small print, and the customer who lost thousands of pounds after inadvertently dialling a hotline to scammers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The progress of a house in London's St John's Wood that sells for well over a million pounds, a terrace in Birkenhead near Liverpool and a piece of land in Handsworth, Birmingham

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, a call about a possible person in the Clyde triggers a huge emergency response, while in Edinburgh, the Special Operations team are called to a fire in a block of flats

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks after proceedings as Danny Sebastian and David Harper help two teams find items in Dorking in Surrey, hoping to make a profit at an auction in Maidenhead

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem, Emlyn and Owen fight for survival after they are trapped deep underground following an unexplained accident at the mine. Kamini is left to deal with patients at the surgery, and Basil's continuing drive for new home buyers begins to reap rewards. Meanwhile, Robert makes the most of Emyln's absence by taking Megan out on a sentimental journey

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Britain's love of cheese has evolved over centuries, travelling to Scotland to sample some of its finest varieties and discovering that the original cheesecake recipes were not American but British

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a house-hunting mission with a dynamic couple in the Herefordshire countryside, hoping they fall in love with the homes they see for their £565,000 budget. Along the way, the presenter heads to the banks of the River Wye to gets to grips with paddle boarding, an ever growing low-impact water sport

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden for Neil and Jack, two friends living together in Leeds who want a space to entertain their guests

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the day of reckoning for Whitney as she receives her verdict. Denise meets up with Ellie to tell her Phil is Raymond's father - and that he is bad news. Shirley and Linda discuss Gray's odd behaviour after he lost his temper, while Stacey struggles to give Ruby and Martin a good enough excuse as to why she was hiding in the office, and tensions between the two women quickly escalate

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Laurence asks Kris to fight in a charity boxing match to raise cash for a homeless shelter. Can he make the leap from mild-mannered hotel manager to prizefighter? VIP guest Louise Brookes checks in, asking for the same room she stayed in 175 times while she attended the inquest into the Hillsborough disaster, and Ciara launches a new competition to promote weddings at the hotel

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker, Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former footballer Dion Dublin, three-time BMX world champion Shanaze Reade, Olympic athlete Iwan Thomas and England rugby union winger Marland Yarde

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets the residents of town in Kent that's slowly sliding into the sea, to see how they are coping with their perilous situation. Plus, the automatic bill switching services that have left some customers wondering whether they'd have been better off doing it themselves

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three flats in a terrible state of repair in a corner property in Wolverhampton and two terraces in Mountain Ash in Wales and Chatham in Kent become available on the market. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell take a look and follow progress as one seasoned developer and two novices tackle their projects

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the Mackintosh at the Willow tearooms in Glasgow to celebrate all things 20th century and collectable. Plus, expert Ben Cooper gives the lowdown on his family connection with Whitefriars glass and Tim Weeks shows off some collectable tech

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem and Kamini uncover the real plan behind New Trefelin, and it begins to look as if foul play may be the cause of the mine's collapse. If it's no accident, Aled and Daf are poised to become the prime suspects - but they protest their innocence. Meanwhile, Robert pleads with Megan to leave the village with him, but Prem confronts him with the truth about his past and Basil's real intentions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: In the final instalment of their culinary journey through the history of British cuisine, Si King and Dave Myers explore comfort food, discussing how certain ingredients and recipes can give people a sense of well-being, such as chicken noodle soup and a simple fish dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Carl Hutchinson present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo, actor Tom Davis, actor and musician Shaun Ryder and comedian Henning Wehn, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is furious with Lola for telling Denise he has been seeing Raymond. Peter gives Kathy's dating app a little tweak in the hope she will have more luck. Sharon worries that Bobby's fears may be getting out of control, while Kush is forced to pay up in exchange for his silence

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts the satirical quiz, inviting contemporary artist and writer Grayson Perry and comedian, actress and writer Janey Godley to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Legendary football manager Arsene Wenger talks about his autobiography My Life in Red and White, while Hollywood star Samuel L Jackson - joined by his wife LaTanya Richardson Jackson - discusses presenting the documentary Enslaved: The Lost History of the Transatlantic Slave Trade. Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora impulsively dyes her hair after a fight with her father, and Wally goes to a single-parent support group meeting. Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Nora suffers a vaginal injury, she's tapped by a DJ to record her chronic queefing for his new track. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Special episode looking at knife crime in the UK, including a dedicated police task force in Essex and a groundbreaking scheme in the West Midlands

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze race horse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and greengrocer Chris Bavin return to help more families with their food shopping, beginning with the Macbeth family in Windsor. Single mum Holly has her hands full looking after her two sons - 16-year-old Spencer and Fletcher, who is 11. Caring for Spencer, who has cerebral palsy, leaves Holly little time to cook, and as a result the family has become dependent on microwavable ready meals. While Spencer is happy to eat most foods, Fletcher - who has recently been diagnosed with autism - is fussier

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Art expert Kate Bliss, trader Linda Lambert and upholsterer Anthony Devine scour the antique shops, scrapyards and flea markets of Monmouth in search of items to make them a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After Woody sees George dancing with Tracy and gets the wrong end of the stick, he hands George an unitemised bill for $40,000 instead of telling him how he feels. It's left to George to work out what's really going on and try to make Woody and Tracy say what normally goes unsaid

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Having settled into his new role as host, Joe Lycett returns, along with the nine remaining home-sewers, who this time are tasked with making children's clothes. Challenges include creating hoodies and turning fake fur coats into fancy dress beasts. The final task sees contestants up against the clock as they make made-to-measure dance costumes so the dancing kids can perform The Hornpipe, the Tarantella and Highland dancing down the catwalk. However, one of them will be shown the door at the end of the show

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan joins a group of Bushmen in Botswana's Kalahari Desert to learn their ancient secrets for surviving on foot among wild lions. They teach him how to make fire, find honey, and gather water from desert plants, and the filmmaker watches as they hunt with poisoned arrows and track predatory lions

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot discovers a valuable sculpture at Rochester Cathedral in Kent and Raj Bisram finds a ghostly Victorian photo album

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Westro skipper James West and his crew have been in the North Sea for three days hunting prawns, but so far have struggled to find a catch so he decides to travel 120 miles in the hope of more luck. New recruit Gavin Burnett heads out on Peterhead trawler Ocean Endeavour, with the 17-year-old being given the responsibilty of bringing the fish on board and cooking for the crew. On the night of the Fishing News Awards, James is nominated for Fisherman of the Year, while rookie Gavin is hoping to land the Trainee of the Year awar

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors David Bradley and Nigel Harman travel to Oxfordshire and Somerset in search of antiques, assisted by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Christina Trevanion. Nigel takes a shine to a domestic appliance of yesteryear, and David comes across an item that resonates with one of the characters he has played

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: In the final episode, Mary travels to the Scottish Highlands. Surrounded by magical scenery, snow-capped mountains and frost covered forests, Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a sausage and pepper hotpot, simple twice-baked souffles, and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: This time, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for low cost baking, using ingredients that do not break the bank or compromise on flavour. Dishes include fluffy onion pretzels, a potato rosti quiche, rhubarb and custard kisses, and her signature rich and indulgent chocolate fondant

programme.title: A Life on Screen
programme.desc: From his early days in comedy troupe The Jockeys of Norfolk, to his breakthrough role as Charles in Four Weddings and a Funeral, Hugh Grant has become one of Britain's best-loved stars, with hits including two Bridget Jones movies, Notting Hill, Love Actually, and Paddington 2. More recently, he has turned his talents to television in the acclaimed drama A Very English Scandal, playing notorious politician Jeremy Thorpe. Aside from the Bafta-winning actor discussing his career, friends and colleagues including Andie MacDowell, Richard Curtis, Sandra Bullock, Colin Firth, Ang Lee, Stephen Frears and Nicholas Hoult also add their thoughts

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Everyone is delighted when Kevin books a cottage for them to spend half term in the countryside with all their children. But the house is not as nice as the pictures online suggested, and it is not long before tempers begin to fray. Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Paul Ready star

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at Fogo Island Inn, which is sited off the coast of Newfoundland in Canada and pays homage to the settlers who arrived from Ireland and Devon

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Nearly five years on from Jim Foley's execution at the hands of Jihadi John, Diane Foley is fighting for justice, while the Muellers continue their search for answers about their missing daughter Kayla. In London a court case in the UK Supreme Court threatens Diane's campaign to see the suspects face a trial in the US, while the Muellers are conducting their own investigation, supported by contacts working secretly in Syria

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Irish resort of Skerries, the crew run to the station when strong winds sweep a teenager on a paddleboard towards the busy shipping lanes of the Irish Sea, while in the Bristol Channel, among the many yachts taking advantage of a fresh summer breeze, one loses a crew member overboard. While the crowds are heading home at the end of a busy summer's day in Cleethorpes, two 13-year-old girls find themselves alone, cut off and at risk of drowning on a sandbank rapidly disappearing under a fast-rising tide

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Chris Wilson re-examines the case of Harold Shipman, exploring how attitudes to the elderly enabled the GP to get away with the murder of hundreds of patients. In the first instalment, he reveals how the doctor's crimes were first discovered and how he was finally apprehended and charged with the murder of 15 of his elderly patients.

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters are shocked when they are attacked by the residents of a house on fire as they try to put it out, while West Midlands Police officers patrol Birmingham on Black Friday

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: How groundbreaking research into the psychology of crowds could help prevent attacks on police. Plus, a report on a surge in coughing attacks on police officers

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including movie posters, Scandinavian furniture and some unusual dolls' heads. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham prepare for a visit from the vet, who is coming to take blood samples from the herd to test for a chronic wasting disease. In Northumberland, Emma's six-month-old puppy Grey is introduced to sheep for the first time, while in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are dealing with an unexpected new arrival. On Little Rowater Farm in Aberdeenshire, a deer calf shows signs of being bullied by the herd

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: In Derbyshire, antiques expert Caroline Hawley, trader Danny Sebastian and aeronautical engineer come upcycler Stu Abbott head to Buxton where they go head to head as they rummage through its antique shops, scrap yards and flea markets in the hope of finding any items that they can each turn into a healthy profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: A car discovered at the bottom of a ravine and is found to contain the body of Bill's long-lost wife Trina. George wants to investigate, but Big Mac stands in his way

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The eight remaining home sewers attempt to revive the glamorous, rebellious and sometimes ridiculous, style of the 1970s. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to create flared jeans, punk-inspired outfits, and maxi dresses. Joe Lycett presents

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan travels to Papua New Guinea, where he meets a tribe on the banks of the Sepik River that has learned to live alongside crocodiles. Members of the tribe teach him to hunt fully grown adult predators using only their bare hands and discovers their belief that a giant crocodile lives in a nearby lake and protects them from harm

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, with brand new valuations and unexpected auctions. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers the fascinating stories of the people of Berwick-upon-Tweed's Paxton House, past and present.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: John Clark steps aside as skipper to let his 22-year-old son David take the helm of fishing boat Reliance for the first time, while Michael Watt is on the hunt for squid in the Moray Firth in his trawler Sardonyx. Back on shore, the Robertsons are checking their boat Zenith into the dry dock for some vital maintenance work

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Cultural review show offering a mixture of interviews, news features and performances from the world of the arts

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Four years after Donald Trump unexpectedly became president of the most powerful nation on earth, this documentary series tells the inside story of this most extraordinary presidency, with contributions from key players including Steve Bannon, Sean Spicer, Omarosa Manigault Newman and Anthony Scaramucci. The first episode charts the first 18 months, covering Trump's change of lifestyle as he moved into the White House, his fraught relationship with the press and his downgrading of White House briefings - preferring instead to talk directly to the public through Twitter

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tries to make sense of the modern world, using a combination of stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction to dissect the week's news - with a panel of guests on hand to take Frankie to task over his claims

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Comedy short featuring Bob Mortimer and Frankie Boyle. Frankie shows Bob his recipe for Lancashire Hot Pot, but runs into trouble when the show fails to deliver its quota of fun, education and social media buzz.

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An alternative nature documentary following the life and struggles of wild wolves as they attempt to hunt down a herd of elk. Narrated by Matt Berry and full of fruity language

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons at Royal Papworth work to completely rebuild the chest wall of a patient who had to have a cancerous lung removed, and has lived with a hole in his back ever since. Consultant thoracic surgeon Aman Coonar and plastic surgeon Charles Malata must take a flap of muscle and skin from Kevin's back and painstakingly attach the blood vessels to fill the cavity and plug the hole. At Addenbrooke, a leading neurosurgeon must remove a potentially fatal tumour from the back of a patient's brain

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian explores how the Romantics created the secular cult of national belonging in their poetry, music and art. In doing so, he explores the genesis of modern nationalism - its Romantic roots in a new idea of nature and homeland; the 'discovery' of native folklore, song and stories and above all the part played by music - especially Frédéric Chopin's mazurkas - in generating the emotion of nationalism. Contributors include singer-songwriter Eddi Reader, German artist Mariele Neudecker and pianist Piotr Anderszewski

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff: Living with Bulimia
programme.desc: The former England cricketer faces up to his eating disorder of 20 years as he challenges the stigma around the condition in men. He explores how bulimia has played a part in the course of his life, discusses his own experience in detail and meets specialists and young men with eating disorders across the UK

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Merseyside police officers attempt a dangerous rescue when a car ends up in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside. Police officers are shocked after an assault in Poole

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a mid 20th-century French cabinet used to display a bride's veil, an unusual wooden children's toy and a round metal case for holding needles and shuttles. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling a set of binoculars salvaged from a U-Boat, two boxing belts awarded to a celebrated Tongan heavyweight, a vintage mechanical money box and an old wooden high chair

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Warrington Wolves v Leeds Rhinos, Hull FC v Huddersfield Giants and St Helens v Wakefield Trinity

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of economic recipes, including a Bake Off masterclass featuring Sussex suet pond pudding, and a James Martin tomato soup with garlic bread

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, market trader Gary Barton and blacksmith upcycler Kevin Paxton head to St Andrews to find some bargains they can turn into profit for Children in Need

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George has a new noisy neighbour, and it is the occupant of the house up the road that he wanted to buy, and the seat of his childhood memories

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The seven home sewers take on man-made fabrics designed for sport and the great outdoors, while the transformation challenge involves discarded festival tents. For the final assignment, male models arrive in the Sewing Room to be fitted with made-to-measure luxury tracksuits. Joe Lycett presents, while Esme Young and Patrick Grant pass judgment

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan sets out to gain a new understanding of fierce predators by meeting different tribes that hunt and live alongside them. He begins on Owarigi in the Solomon Islands, where the local men encounter dangerous sharks almost every day, even swimming alongside them as they freedive for the fish they need to survive

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are at Glemham Hall in the Suffolk countryside, where David Fletcher spots a rare flintlock pistol, and James Lewis uncovers a copy of a Raphael painting

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Mark and Adam Robertson finally get their trawler Zephyr back in the water after a break for repairs, but just 30 miles out they suffer an engine problem and have to turn back to shore. On the Reliance, David Clark is four days through his first trip as skipper when he faces an inspection by Marine Scotland

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson demonstrates dishes that combine the comfort of the familiar with the excitement of the new, including fluffy oat pancakes drizzled with a raspberry and honey syrup. She offers a twist on a classic Caesar salad for a light lunch idea, and rustles up a Greek-inspired supper for friends including squid and orzo pasta, and a sweet and savoury version of a cheesecake. Nigella's simple rice bowl with ginger, radish and avocado celebrates a few choice ingredients and results in a dish that's uplifting to look at, as well as to eat

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedians Lolly Adefope and Ellie Taylor, musician Ricky Wilson and presenter and author Richard Osman, who reveal the books that make them cry and the fictional characters they most relate to. They discuss this week's book group picks - Love After Love by Ingrid Persaud and Richard's debut novel, The Thursday Murder Club

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie heads to a friend's farm where a young calf has developed a serious limp. The calf's protective mother is making it clear she wants her baby back, and Jennie does not want to distress her, so has to work fast. Vet Darren treats a two-day-old lamb, whose owner thinks has a urinary infection. However, when she happily relieves herself all over Darren's examination table, he has to use all his investigative skills to diagnose the real problem. Maurice King, the founder of the Downe Veterinary Clinic, encounters a loggerhead turtle has been rescued by the local sea life sanctuary

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnie's labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don prepares his pond for the season ahead and prunes a climbing rose. Adam Frost offers ideas for a winter display of containers and the programme catches up with Frances Tophill on her allotment in Kent, to find out about her growing year. Plus, another chance to see Carol Klein's autumnal visit to Hidcote Gardens in Gloucestershire, and a couple whose garden has been inspired by the Far East

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Paloma Faith chats with Jools ahead of the release of her fifth album Infinite Things, the follow-up to 2017's number one The Architect. Her forthcoming record was completed before the pandemic, but during lockdown she changed it dramatically to reflect the times. A selection of her earlier work features amid a diverse selection of clips from the show's archive. Plus, a performance at Jools' studio from producer, songwriter and musician Fraser T Smith. Famed for his work with the likes of Adele and rappers Stormzy and Dave, he's now stepping out with his own music. There's also a performance by Malian songwriter and guitarist Afel Bocoum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Roslin fears the worst when a convicted felon is nominated to become the next vice president, and her advisers tell her she must do everything in her power to prevent it from happening. Meanwhile, on Caprica, Helo gets a wake-up call. Mary McDonnell and Tahmoh Penikett star

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Galactica crew finally locates the legendary planet Kobol, where the ancient scrolls say humankind originated. However, as the ship descends to explore the fabled world, Cylon raiders appear and launch a strike - and they have plenty of resources waiting to join the battle

programme.title: The New Girlfriend (2014)
programme.desc: A woman grieving the death of her lifelong best friend gets to know her husband, only to discover he secretly likes wearing his late wife's clothes. She helps him to accept his transvestism, and teaches him to create a new female alter ego for himself. Drama based on a short story by Ruth Rendell, starring Anais Demoustier and Romain Duris. In French

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries focused on families, including his trilogy of documentaries centred around the Westboro Baptist Church. He examines how the death of patriarch Fred Phelps has left members of the church divided. He also reflects on how physical and mental health or forces beyond control can threaten to derail family lives

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: Artist Lachlan Goudie visits the Scottish National Gallery to explore the relevance of collection to the challenges people face today in terms of family, community, nature and hope

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint a rugged group of mountains peering down upon nature's greenery

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts how Edward I turned the castle into an instrument of colonisation. He spent a large amount of money on subduing the Welsh and built fearsome fortresses in Caernarfon, Gwynedd and Beaumaris, which were used to impose England's rule on the country. However, when the king's attention turned to Scotland, his plans didn't go as expected

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Comedy, written and directed by Mike Leigh, following the events of a party held in a suburban house. As the night wears on, the guests become increasingly inebriated and indiscreet, paving the way for shocking revelations and home truths. Alison Steadman, Tim Stern, John Salthouse and Janine Duvitski star

programme.title: Drama Out of a Crisis: A Celebration of Play for Today
programme.desc: Marking the 50th anniversary of the first Play For Today, this film celebrates the acclaimed series of controversial single dramas broadcast on BBC One between 1970 and 1984, told by a number of its producers, directors and writers

programme.title: Abducted - Elizabeth I's Child Actors
programme.desc: Thirteen year-old Thomas Clifton was walking to school in December 1600 when he was violently kidnapped by men who claimed that they had legal authority to do so from Elizabeth I herself. Katherine Rundell examines the case, finding that the snatching of Thomas had been organised by a theatrical impresario who intended to put him on the stage as part of a company of child actors

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: Artist Lachlan Goudie visits the Scottish National Gallery to explore the relevance of collection to the challenges people face today in terms of family, community, nature and hope

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint a rugged group of mountains peering down upon nature's greenery

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts how Edward I turned the castle into an instrument of colonisation. He spent a large amount of money on subduing the Welsh and built fearsome fortresses in Caernarfon, Gwynedd and Beaumaris, which were used to impose England's rule on the country. However, when the king's attention turned to Scotland, his plans didn't go as expected

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: A special-access tour of the National Gallery's Artemisia Gentileschi exhibition, which was billed as Tarantino meets Caravaggio, a bloody tale of tragedy and triumph with a twist

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he captures on canvas the haunting loveliness of stalwart trees shielding a lonesome brook from the light

programme.title: Rio Bravo (1959)
programme.desc: Sheriff John T Chance and his drunken deputy Dude make a courageous stand against a rancher and his hired guns, who are threatening to tear apart a peace-loving town if a murderer being held in the local jail is not released. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Dean Martin, Ricky Nelson, Walter Brennan and Angie Dickinson, who won a Golden Globe for Most Promising Newcomer for her portrayal of Feathers

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the Great Plains - 500,000-square miles of flat, treeless grassland - became the setting for some of the Wild West's most dramatic events. Ray joins members of the Blackfeet Nation as they demonstrate bare-back riding skills before undertaking a ritual buffalo hunt and sacrifice. At a cattle auction in Dodge City, Kansas, he reveals stories about the railways and cow-towns before learning about the life of an iconic figure - the cowboy

programme.title: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: Profile of the enigmatic American musician, who rose to fame in the 1980s with hits including 1999, Kiss and Raspberry Beret. As well as achieving significant commercial success around the world, he won critical acclaim for his adventurous, genre-blurring albums, such as Sign O' the Times and Around the World in a Day - while also making headlines for his sexually explicit lyrics and stage shows, as well as his legal battle to retain control of his name and music. Featuring contributions by guitarist Dez Dickerson, Public Enemy frontman Chuck D, soul singer Beverley Knight and Paisley Park label president Alan Leeds

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The new techniques being pioneered by vision-impaired astronomers at Jodrell Bank to see the universe, using their senses of hearing and touch

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines the golden age of American art, beginning in the Wild West with the art of the Hudson River School, before culminating in a celebration of the work of Jackson Pollock, a native of Wyoming who was heavily influenced by the mood of the American West and the example of Native American artists

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: A special-access tour of the National Gallery's Artemisia Gentileschi exhibition, which was billed as Tarantino meets Caravaggio, a bloody tale of tragedy and triumph with a twist

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he captures on canvas the haunting loveliness of stalwart trees shielding a lonesome brook from the light

At a cattle auction in Dodge City, Kansas, he reveals stories about the railways and cow-towns before learning about the life of an iconic figure - the cowboy

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first broadcast on January 18, featuring performances by Halo James, the Quireboys, 49ers, New Kids on the Block, Megadeth, Kylie Minogue and Martika

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Jackie Brambles host the edition first aired on January 26, with performances by artists including Phil Collins, Public Enemy, Sinead O'Connor, Del Amitri, Kylie Minogue and Yell!

programme.title: Summer Holiday (1963)
programme.desc: Four mechanics embark on a fun-filled trip across Europe on board a double-decker London bus, and enjoy all manner of romantic adventures with three young women who hitch a ride on their way to Athens. However, an unexpected stowaway causes a few surprises. Musical comedy, starring Cliff Richard, Lauri Peters, Melvyn Hayes, Una Stubbs, Ron Moody and the Shadows

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard at the BBC
programme.desc: As the Peter Pan of Pop reaches his 80th birthday, a look back through its archives at some of his most memorable performances and biggest hits. Amongst the classic songs featured are Move It, The Young Ones, Living Doll, Bachelor Boy, the Eurovision Song Contest contenders Congratulations and Power to All Our Friends, Devil Woman, Miss You Nights, Wired for Sound and Cliff's much-praised '80s duet with Van Morrison, Wherever God Shines His Light

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Archive clips featuring solo artists, including music by performers as diverse as Sandie Shaw, Dusty Springfield, Long John Baldry, Lulu, Tom Jones, Brenton Wood, Cliff Richard, Cilla Black and Peter Sarstedt

programme.title: From Elton John to Gary Barlow: Celebrating 100 Concerts Live at Eden
programme.desc: A look back over the last 18 years of the Eden Sessions. Introduced by Nile Rodgers, who headlined the 100th show, highlights include Pulp, who headlined the first one in July 2002. Set against the backdrop of the Eden biomes, there are also performances by Duran Duran, Bastille, Muse, Gary Barlow, Van Morrison, Madness, Primal Scream and Lionel Richie

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard at the BBC
programme.desc: As the Peter Pan of Pop reaches his 80th birthday, a look back through its archives at some of his most memorable performances and biggest hits. programme.desc: Temperatures rise as the zoo is hit by a heatwave. Many of the animals are well adapted to the sun, but those from cooler climates need extra attention. The boys devise a series of ingenious tricks to keep each animal cool, including ice baths, mist showers and giant ice lollies. It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. Meanwhile, the camels get their yearly comb-down, Tyler has to muck out the stinky Penguin pool and the boys play with an orphaned elephant in Uganda.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Laurence asks Kris to fight in a charity boxing match to raise cash for a homeless shelter. Can he make the leap from mild-mannered hotel manager to prizefighter? VIP guest Louise Brookes checks in, asking for the same room she stayed in 175 times while she attended the inquest into the Hillsborough disaster, and Ciara launches a new competition to promote weddings at the hotel

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.desc: Sue Barker, Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former footballer Dion Dublin, three-time BMX world champion Shanaze Reade, Olympic athlete Iwan Thomas and England rugby union winger Marland Yarde

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets the residents of town in Kent that's slowly sliding into the sea, to see how they are coping with their perilous situation. Plus, the automatic bill switching services that have left some customers wondering whether they'd have been better off doing it themselves

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three flats in a terrible state of repair in a corner property in Wolverhampton and two terraces in Mountain Ash in Wales and Chatham in Kent become available on the market. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell take a look and follow progress as one seasoned developer and two novices tackle their projects

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem and Kamini uncover the real plan behind New Trefelin, and it begins to look as if foul play may be the cause of the mine's collapse. If it's no accident, Aled and Daf are poised to become the prime suspects - but they protest their innocence. Meanwhile, Robert pleads with Megan to leave the village with him, but Prem confronts him with the truth about his past and Basil's real intentions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: In the final instalment of their culinary journey through the history of British cuisine, Si King and Dave Myers explore comfort food, discussing how certain ingredients and recipes can give people a sense of well-being, such as chicken noodle soup and a simple fish dish

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the Mackintosh at the Willow tearooms in Glasgow to celebrate all things 20th century and collectable. Plus, expert Ben Cooper gives the lowdown on his family connection with Whitefriars glass and Tim Weeks shows off some collectable tech

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Carl Hutchinson present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond joins chef Nick Nairn at one the most beautiful fishing locations in Scotland, the Lake of Menteith. The duo fish for rainbow trout then cook a delicious dish by the side of the lake. Anne Lundon investigates the need to adapt the landscape as extreme weather events become more frequent amid climate change. In Dundee, Arlene Stuart meets a furniture-maker who crafts pieces from brewing waste. Plus, entomologist Gabby Flynn reveals why wasps are vital for the ecosystem

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is furious with Lola for telling Denise he has been seeing Raymond. Peter gives Kathy's dating app a little tweak in the hope she will have more luck. Sharon worries that Bobby's fears may be getting out of control, while Kush is forced to pay up in exchange for his silence

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts the satirical quiz, inviting contemporary artist and writer Grayson Perry and comedian, actress and writer Janey Godley to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Legendary football manager Arsene Wenger talks about his autobiography My Life in Red and White, while Hollywood star Samuel L Jackson - joined by his wife LaTanya Richardson Jackson - discusses presenting the documentary Enslaved: The Lost History of the Transatlantic Slave Trade. Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora impulsively dyes her hair after a fight with her father, and Wally goes to a single-parent support group meeting. Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Nora suffers a vaginal injury, she's tapped by a DJ to record her chronic queefing for his new track. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: A View from the Terrace

Actress and comedian Dawn French promotes her new novel Because of You, and cricketer and Top Gear presenter Freddie Flintoff reflects on his new book Right, Said Fred, which mocks the absurdities of modern life. Plus, recent Mercury Prize-winner Michael Kiwanuka performs his current single Light and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora impulsively dyes her hair after a fight with her father, and Wally goes to a single-parent support group meeting. Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Nora suffers a vaginal injury, she's tapped by a DJ to record her chronic queefing for his new track. Awkwafina stars

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at those challenges when the candidates were told to gather their passports as Alan Sugar sent them to conduct business abroad, Apprentice-style. But these short breaks have been anything but a holiday for the entrepreneurs as they face all manner of obstacles, from language barriers to a lack of local knowledge. Over the years, the over-confident contestants have tried to sell cheese to the French, fumbled their facts in Bruges and confused a slimy sea creature for scuba equipment in Malta. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of five colleagues answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tries various ways to get the money together and Paul wonders how he can help, while Aaron has an altercation with Ben's friend and leaves. Marlon is shocked when Bear tells him Chas was caught flirting with Al, and Vinny is stunned by what he discovers about Liv

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: George reveals his plan to stage a mock funeral so that Mick will think Todd is dead, and the Platts prepare for Audrey's belated 80th birthday party. Tyrone is puzzled when Evelyn and Arthur return back early from the Lakes with a story full of holes, and Scott orders Johnny to meet him to talk him through his plan

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Vietnam Airlines are battling baggage woes in the lead up to the busy holiday period, and rookie engineer Mo comes up with an ingenious solution to ensure no one is ever stranded abroad without their luggage

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd puts Eileen in danger when he lets himself into Number 11 and starts searching for her cash, while Gary tells Ray that the factory is not for sale. Scott tells Johnny that his job is to drive the getaway car and if he doesn't comply, he'll reveal all to Jenny, and Evelyn tells Arthur that she's sorry she ruined their trip with her sleeping arrangements

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. This time, the hotel hosts a series of BAFTA-related events, which sees a multitude of celebrities and film stars from across the globe coming in and out of the hotel. Head butler Sean shows newest butler Michael the ropes about discretion and how to look after the hotel's most important guests

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: UEFA Nations League Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the fourth round of fixtures, including England v Denmark at Wembley Stadium, Scotland v Czech Republic at Hampden Park, Bulgaria v Wales at Vasil Levski National Stadium and Norway v Northern Ireland at Ullevaal Stadion. With analysis from Roy Keane and Ian Wright and commentary by Sam Matterface, Lee Dixon, Joe Speight, Seb Hutchinson, Joshua Adu-Donkor and Tom Gayle

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads towards Louisiana's swamplands, where he ditches his car for a high-speed boat. Then, he travels to the nearby city of Lafayette, where he visits the family run smokehouse, Johnson's Boucaniere, and samples some of their traditional homemade sausage. James also tries ice cream from the legendary Borden's ice cream parlour, before heading back to the swamps to cook one final dish

programme.title: Tenable
